scenario.context: Station 2 of the daily run: clarify information security basics and roles.
scenario.mission: You classify protection goals, roles, and reporting paths for your work area.

station.profile: Information assets, protection goals, and roles in daily operations

customerRequest.subject: Station 02: prepare security basics for the work area
customerRequest.body: Please create a compact overview for new colleagues on protection goals, roles, and reporting paths in daily security work.

questions.title: Core of information security
questions.prompt: Which combination forms the core of information security?

options.rationale: These protection goals form the classic security base.
These are not information security goals.

feedback.correct: Correct: These goals drive security assessment.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: use the classic protection goals.

questions.title: Roles in the security process
questions.prompt: Which statements about roles and responsibilities are sensible? (Multiple selections)

options.text: The business unit describes business criticality

options.rationale: Technical implementation typically sits with IT.

options.text: Reporting paths for incidents should be known to everyone involved
options.rationale: Only then are incidents escalated in time.

options.text: Documentation is optional in security cases
Each person decides alone on protection need
options.rationale: Protection need requires alignment with business unit and IT.

feedback.correct: Correct: Roles, reporting paths, and evidence are assigned cleanly.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: classify roles, reporting paths, and documentation more clearly.

questions.title: Reporting path for a security suspicion
items: Use security contacts or reporting path
correctOrder: Use security contacts or reporting path

feedback.correct: Correct: This keeps the reporting path controlled and traceable.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: sequence detect, secure, report, and document cleanly.

questions.title: Share with high confidentiality need
questions.prompt: Out of 16 workstations, 6 have a high confidentiality need. What is the share in percent?

feedback.wrong: Divide the number with high need by the total and convert to percent.

questions.title: Explain security fundamentals
questions.prompt: Explain in 4-6 sentences why protection goals, roles, and reporting paths belong together in daily work.
questions.expectedOutline: Explain protection goals, separate roles, and classify reporting as part of the process.

feedback.correct: Correct: The relationship is clear and practical.
feedback.wrong: Please link protection goals, roles, and reporting path more clearly.
